content:
# Disagreements — Genesis 35 (GEN_035)

## 1. El Shaddai — Translated vs. Untranslated (v. 11)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "El Shaddai" (untranslated) | Project convention; etymology unresolved |
| LOGOS | "El Shaddai" (untranslated) | Project convention; retains patriarchal divine-name tradition |
| PNEUMA | "El Shaddai — God Almighty" | Adds traditional rendering for reader accessibility |
| **SOPHIA** | **"El Shaddai" (untranslated) — 2-1** | Convention established at Gen 17:1, 28:3. LXX omits Shaddai entirely, confirming ancient uncertainty. |

## 2. "And Israel heard" — Object Supplied or Omitted (v. 22)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "and Israel heard" | No object in Hebrew; silence is the text's meaning |
| LOGOS | "And Israel heard of it" | Supplies implied object for English readability |
| PNEUMA | "And Israel heard about it" | Supplies implied object for English readability |
| **SOPHIA** | **"And Israel heard." — EMET upheld** | The objectless verb + setumah paragraph break = deliberate literary silence. Adding "of it" diminishes the effect. |

## 3. "Full of days" vs. "Full of years" (v. 29)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "full of days" | Literal: yamim = days |
| LOGOS | "full of days" | Standard patriarchal formula (cf. Gen 25:8) |
| PNEUMA | "full of years" | Interpretive equivalent for modern reader |
| **SOPHIA** | **"full of days" — 2-1** | Fixed formula across patriarchal death notices. Consistency with Gen 25:8 (Abraham) requires "days." |

## 4. Ben-oni Gloss (v. 18)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "Ben-oni [son of my sorrow / son of my strength]" | Both etymologies preserved |
| LOGOS | "Ben-oni" (no gloss in text) | Transliteration only; notes cover meaning |
| PNEUMA | "Ben-oni: Son of My Sorrow" | Primary etymology selected by context |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Ben-oni [Son of My Sorrow]" — contextual reading adopted** | Death context decisively favors sorrow reading. LXX confirms ("Son of my pain"). EMET's dual-etymology note remains valid as scholarly reference. |

## 5. Kivrat-ha'arets — Transliterated vs. Interpreted (v. 16)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a kivrat-ha'arets [a stretch of land...]" | Preserves the untranslatable term |
| LOGOS | "some distance" | Communicates narrative sense without false precision |
| PNEUMA | "some distance" | Same as LOGOS |
| **SOPHIA** | **"some distance" — 2-1** | Hapax legomenon with no recoverable meaning. "Some distance" serves the narrative without misleading. EMET's preservation of the Hebrew term is noted for scholarly use. |

## 6. "God went up" vs. "God withdrew" (v. 13)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "God went up from upon him" | Literal: alah = ascend; me'alav = from upon him |
| LOGOS | "God went up from him" | Preserves upward motion; slightly simplified |
| PNEUMA | "God withdrew from the place" | Dynamic equivalent; loses spatial theology |
| **SOPHIA** | **"God went up from him" — LOGOS formulation, 2-1** | The Hebrew verb alah specifically denotes ascent. "Withdrew" erases the vertical cosmology. LOGOS's formulation preserves the upward motion while remaining readable. |

## 7. Earrings Interpolation (v. 4)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the rings that were in their ears" | Text only; amulet interpretation in notes |
| LOGOS | "the rings that were in their ears" | Text only; cultic function discussed in notes |
| PNEUMA | "the rings they wore in their ears — for these were pagan amulets" | Interpretive gloss added to text |
| **SOPHIA** | **No interpolation — 2-1** | The cultic function of the earrings is a valid inference but belongs in commentary, not translation text. |

## 8. Massebah Convention (vv. 14, 20)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "standing-pillar [מַצֵּבָה]" | Hebrew term with English gloss |
| LOGOS | "pillar" / "pillar of stone" | English translation only |
| PNEUMA | "stone pillar" / "pillar" | English translation only |
| **SOPHIA** | **"massebah [standing-pillar]" — convention applied** | Project convention requires massebah preserved with bracketed gloss. Applied to both v. 14 (Bethel) and v. 20 (Rachel's grave). |

## 9. Chittat Elohim — Construct Preserved vs. Interpreted (v. 5)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a terror of God" | Construct form preserved |
| LOGOS | "a terror from God" | Divine-source genitive interpretation |
| PNEUMA | "a terror from God" | Same as LOGOS |
| **SOPHIA** | **"a terror of God" — EMET upheld** | The Hebrew construct chittat Elohim is ambiguous between superlative and source genitive. "Of God" preserves both readings; "from God" decides for one. |

---

*Genesis 35: 9 disagreements logged. SOPHIA ruled with EMET on 4 items (vv. 5, 14/20, 22, earrings), with LOGOS on 1 item (v. 13), with EMET/LOGOS jointly on 3 items (vv. 11, 29, earrings), and adopted a contextual reading for 1 item (v. 18 Ben-oni). PNEUMA was overruled on 5 items (vv. 4, 11, 13, 22, 29).*
